name: John Mehegan
abstract: John Francis Mehegan (June 6, 1916 – April 3, 1984) was an American jazz pianist, lecturer and critic.

concerts.label: Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Ellen Rowe
about_quotes.evidence: what really turned the corner for me I think was when I got to study with John Mehegan
about_quotes.doc_title: Ellen Rowe interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 2, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Ridley
about_quotes.evidence: the David Bakers and the Jerry Cokers and the John Mehegan books... these are the guys that were at the very beginning of it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: That fits right in with John Mehegan saying, 'Marian, there’s not enough Bird in your playing.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
